title: dactylis smithii subsp. marina
intro: Dactylis smithii subsp. marina, also known as sea cockspur or coastal bent grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylis smithii subsp. marina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
